meta.prompt: Add Remote Access moment — tunneling options (Tailscale Funnel, ngrok, Cloudflare, VPS) for exposing local Sentinel ports to Claude CoWork and remote AI agents

title: Developer Workflow
subtitle: From scaffold to always-on service — three distinct moments
intro: AppySentinel separates scaffold time, build time, and deploy time. Each moment has a clear boundary. The CLI handles Scaffold automatically. Build is iterative developer work. Deploy is a one-command registration with the host OS.

moments.label: Scaffold
moments.description: The CLI copies the template, substitutes project name, runs bun install and git init. No prompts for machine name — that comes from os.hostname() at runtime.

what_you_get.note: AppySentinel conventions baked in — recipe pattern, Signal envelope, three umbrellas, hard rules
Registers as launchd (macOS) or systemd (Linux). Detects OS automatically.
Clean removal from the host OS service manager
macOS template — PROJECT_NAME, PROJECT_DIR, BUN_PATH substituted at install time
MACHINE_NAME is a commented-out override. Default is os.hostname() — no action required.
Project name, bun scripts (dev/start/test/test:watch/typecheck/prepare), @appydave/appysentinel-core dependency wired to published version
Vitest config — node environment, globals, 10s timeout. Ready to run with bun run test.
Smoke test — starts the sentinel with installSignalHandlers: false, asserts lifecycle reaches running then stopped cleanly.
Pre-push hook — runs bun run test && bun run typecheck before every git push. Installed automatically on bun install via prepare script.

moments.note: After Scaffold the project is functional but unconfigured. main.ts emits one sentinel.started event then idles. Wire recipes before it does anything useful.

moments.label: Build / Develop
moments.description: The iterative development phase. Wire collectors, storage, and expose surfaces into main.ts. Use watch mode to get instant feedback.

commands.purpose: Install dependencies after scaffold or after adding a recipe dep
Run the Sentinel directly — structured logs to stdout
Compile TypeScript to dist/ — needed before service registration
Run Vitest tests once. In a scaffolded project: runs the smoke test + any tests you add. In the AppySentinel monorepo: runs all 46 tests (39 core + 7 CLI).
Watch mode — Vitest re-runs only tests affected by changed files. Leave open while wiring recipes.

pre_push_hook.command: bun run test && bun run typecheck
pre_push_hook.note: Ships in every scaffolded project (.husky/pre-push) and in the AppySentinel monorepo. Installed automatically when bun install runs the prepare script. Broken code cannot reach the remote. Do not bypass with --no-verify — fix the failure instead.

moments.label: Deploy
moments.description: Register the built Sentinel as a host OS service. It starts at login, restarts on crash, and runs unattended. No manual supervision.

commands.purpose: Detect OS, substitute placeholders, register with launchd or systemd
Remove from host OS service manager cleanly

moments.label: Remote Access
moments.tagline: Expose local ports to remote AI agents.
moments.description: Sentinels bind to localhost by default. Claude CoWork and other remote AI agents require an https:// URL — they cannot reach localhost directly. Use a tunnel to bridge the gap without changing the Sentinel's local-first design.
moments.note: The Sentinel itself needs no changes. The tunnel proxies from the public URL to the local port. Disable the tunnel when not in use.

options.note: Recommended. Already in use across the AppyDave fleet. One command, persistent stable URL. Works immediately with CoWork. Disable with: tailscale funnel --bg <PORT> off
URL changes on restart (free tier). Useful for quick one-off testing.
Free stable subdomain. More configuration required. Worth it for persistent multi-machine setups.
Full control. Overkill for personal fleet tools.

ci_pipeline.title: CI / CD Pipeline
ci_pipeline.description: GitHub Actions. One workflow handles everything — no manual steps.

workflows.name: CI + Publish
workflows.trigger: Every push to main + pull requests
steps: Auto-tag vX.Y.Z if version is new (main push only)
Publish each package to npm — idempotent (skips if version already exists)
workflows.note: Publish steps run only on main push, not PRs. GITHUB_TOKEN tag pushes don't trigger other workflows (GitHub security guard) — so publish runs inline after the tag step. Requires NPM_TOKEN secret: granular access token covering all three packages + appydave org. Expires ~90 days — rotate before expiry.
